summary: Gruber highlights how David Smith's Widgetsmith app became a massive hit as iOS 14 widgets took off, only to be undermined by a shameless App Store rip-off that Apple approved and even ranked above the original in search results. The rip-off app copied both the name and icon of Widgetsmith, yet passed App Store review and appeared as the top search result despite the real app being #1 in Productivity with 53,000+ reviews. Gruber uses this as a case study to challenge Apple's claims that the App Store is a 'safe and trusted place,' arguing that Apple's own curation failures contradict the very narrative Apple is promoting to justify its App Store control. He draws an analogy to Apple's retail stores, noting they would never let knockoff products displace genuine ones on their shelves. The piece was updated to note the rip-off was removed two hours later, but the systemic problem remains.
key_points: A blatant Widgetsmith rip-off was approved by App Store review with a near-identical name and icon, and ranked above the real app in search results
Apple's App Store review process failed at multiple levels: approving the clone, ranking it above the original, and not proactively catching the fraud
Apple's simultaneous PR campaign touting the App Store as 'safe and trusted' is directly contradicted by cases like this
The App Store's search algorithm is fundamentally broken if a 25-rating knockoff can outrank a #1 app with 53,000+ reviews
Apple would never tolerate knockoff products in its retail stores, yet routinely allows them in the App Store
money_quotes: It wouldn't be more obvious that this app is a rip-off if its name were 'Widgetsmith - Rip-Off Version'.
The App Store is not trustworthy if that includes trusting that the apps in its trending lists and search results are legitimate.
If Apple ran a food court like they run the App Store they'd let a McDowell's open up two stores down from McDonald's.

I'm pretty sure that if you go to Apple's online store and search for 'Solo Loop', you're not going to be presented with a fly-by-night piece-of-crap knockoff named 'Solo Loop - Color Bands', with Apple's actual Solo Loops hidden behind them.
I doubt anyone feels good about 'Widgetsmith - Rip-Off Version', including the hucksters who made it.
Where's the App Store bunco squad? This wouldn't even be a hard case to crack.
key_insight: Apple's claim that the App Store is a curated, trustworthy marketplace rings hollow when its review process approves obvious rip-offs and its search algorithm ranks them above the originals.
